'''''Star Wars: Republic Commando'': Prima Official Game Guide''' is a [[Star Wars video games|computer game]] strategy guide for ''[[Star Wars: Republic Commando]]'', written by [[Michael Knight]] and published by [[Prima Games]] in [[2005]].

==Publisher's summary==
''*''Detailed strategies for all 3 campaigns of precise military-style operations''''
''*''Extensive maps for every mission''''
''*''Comprehensive stats for all weapons and equipment''''
''*''Enemies exposed: a complete breakdown of each enemy's weapons and weaknesses''''
''*''Multiplayer tips and tactics''''
''*''Exclusive art gallery—a look behind the graphics''''

==Contents==
The Game guide opened with the Prologue from the [[2004]] novel ''[[Republic Commando: Hard Contact]]'', a tie-in to the [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Republic Commando]]''. Told from the perspective of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Republic]] [[Clone commando/Legends|Commando]] [[Theta Squad]] member [[Darman Skirata|Darman]], who retells their first and last mission to the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Geonosis/Legends|Geonosis]]—the squad quickly gets overwhelmed and, save for Darman, they all [[Death/Legends|perish]]. The prologue is followed by a short "How to use this guide" section, outlining the contents of the guide and the purpose of each section. The guide also includes the one-page long short story [[The Tale of the Aiwha Pod (short story)|''The Tale of the Aiwha Pod'']] written by [[Ryan Kaufman]].<ref name="guide" />

==Development==
The game guide, written by [[Michael Knight]], was released by [[Prima Games]] on [[March 8]], [[2005]] in paperback format. The book's dimensions were 8.3 x 0.3 x 10.7 inches and it had 128 pages, weighing at 8.8 ounces.<ref name="Amazon" /> Designed by [[Simon Olney]] and [[Derek Hocking]], the product manager was [[Mario De Govia]] with [[Christy Seifert]] as the editorial supervisor and [[Rebecca Chastain]] editing the work. Various concept artwork and screenshots/game models were used throughout the guide.<ref name="guide" />
